action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 24 achieved a new best test AUC of 0.7658 with a 15-model ensemble (ensemble_15). Remaining time is only ~2.3 minutes with 6 iterations left—too tight for training new models from scratch (3 models take ~2 min). Analysis: Rather than risking a timeout by training more models for diminishing returns, I can improve the existing ensemble by optimizing combination weights on the validation set. Current predict.py uses val_auc as a proxy for weighting, but weights that minimize validation BCE (or maximize validation AUC directly) often produce better test performance. Because the models are already trained, this costs only inference time (~10-20 s) and is very safe timewise. Decision: Write optimize_bce.py to load every model in ensemble_15/, compute validation predictions, find weights that minimize BCE (and compare with uniform and val_auc weighting), save the best weights.json into ensemble_15/, and update predict.py to read weights.json when present. Then submit eval on ensemble_15/.
